summary: Map the numpair -> ind_type -> Datatype build closure on /tmp/hol4_defn (4 parallel recon agents)

result.layer: numpairTheory (Stage 6c) on /tmp/hol4_defn — the N×N→N bijection (triangular numbers tri/invtri, npair/nfst/nsnd)
result.summary:
numpairScript.sml is a small (325-line), almost-entirely-arithmetic theory: 6 Definitions + 34 Theorems + an Overload/fixity/Unicode block. I read the whole script and probed /tmp/hol4_defn end-to-end. VERDICT: buildable via a numeral_sweep-style per-chunk Script sweep on /tmp/hol4_defn, with ONE genuine engineering keystone and a handful of mechanical shims — all of which I validated live on the checkpoint.

KEY FINDINGS (all confirmed by running poly on /tmp/hol4_defn):
1. numLib is NOT needed. ARITH_TAC/DECIDE/numLib.* are MISSING, but the script never calls ARITH_TAC/DECIDE directly — it uses SRW_TAC [ARITH_ss], and numSimps.ARITH_ss + numSimps.REDUCE_ss + reduceLib ARE present. The script's own `val DECIDE_TAC = SRW_TAC [ARITH_ss][]` (line 41) re-defines DECIDE_TAC as a weak alias, so the missing real DECIDE is irrelevant. Do NOT load numLib (would pull whileTheory/numeral closure unnecessarily).
2. THE KEYSTONE: `Definition invtri0_def` (line 72) is non-structural recursion `invtri0 n a = if n<a+1 then (n,a) else invtri0 (n-(a+1)) (a+1)` with NO Termination block in source — upstream relies on Define's auto-termination guesser, which our shimmed PRIM_STP_TAC cannot do (probed: "Unable to automatically prove termination"). TWO sub-fixes needed, BOTH VALIDATED LIVE:
   (a) Register `pair` in TypeBase (pair is NOT registered; only num is). Mirror build_defn's num registration: TypeBasePure.gen_datatype_info {ax=pairTheory.pair_Axiom, ind=pairTheory.pair_induction, case_defs=[pairTheory.pair_case_def]} then TypeBase.write. Without this, even Pmatch fails ("Pattern (n,a) is not a constructor"). Confirmed it then accepts the paired domain.
   (b) Override the invtri0_def chunk to use TotalDefn.tDefine "invtri0" <frags> <termTac> where termTac (VALIDATED to fully close + register invtri0_ind) = WF_REL_TAC `measure FST` THEN REWRITE_TAC [prim_recTheory.measure_thm, pairTheory.FST] THEN REPEAT STRIP_TAC THEN BasicProvers.FULL_SIMP_TAC (srw_ss()++ARITH_ss) [arithmeticTheory.NOT_LESS]. The NOT_LESS + the sweep-shim's assumption-injecting FULL_SIMP_TAC is what closes the final `n-(a+1)<n` (the stock simp drops the assumption a+1<=n). Confirmed `invtri0_ind` IS registered and HO_MATCH_MP_TAC (theorem "invtri0_ind") applies.
3. `theorem "invtri0_ind"` (used lines 86,95) — top-level `theorem`/Theory.theorem is MISSING. Inject a helper `val theorem = fn nm => #2 (valOf (List.find (fn(n,_)=>n=nm)(Theory.current_theorems()@Theory.current_definitions())))` in the rebind shim. Validated it fetches invtri0_ind.
4. Everything else is routine: tri (structural Define, [simp]); invtri/npair/nfst/nsnd (non-recursive Define); 34 arithmetic Theorems proved by Induct_on/Cases_on/SRW_TAC[ARITH_ss]/METIS_TAC/HO_MATCH_MP_TAC. Validated twotri_formula proves via Induct_on `n` THEN SRW_TAC[ARITH_ss][...]. The BasicProvers shim's Cases_on(num via num_CASES)/Induct_on(INDUCT_THEN numTheory.INDUCTION) from numeral_sweep cover the inductions.
5. Overload/fixity/Unicode/TeX block (lines 79,171-176): Parse.set_fixity "*," (Infixr 601), Unicode.unicode_version, TeX_notation, Overload "*,"=``npair`` — ALL validated working (Overload syntax is quote-filter-expanded to Parse.overload_on; backtick ``npair`` becomes a frag list via the filter). UnicodeChars.sup_minus/sup_1, UTF8.chr present.
6. 8 METIS_TAC uses across 7 theorems (each ≤3 lemmas) — small, but watch the documented mlib time-slice Time-exception on cumulative proving; per-chunk fail-and-continue handles it (re-run flaky chunks). Tactic combinators >>/>-/\\\\ are present in boolLib/Tactical but need infix declarations added to the sweep header (numeral_sweep declares only THEN/THENL/THEN1/ORELSE/by).
modules: BASE: /tmp/hol4_defn (has arithmeticTheory, numeralTheory, numSimps.ARITH_ss, reduceLib, TotalDefn.Define, pairTheory, prim_recTheory, metisLib/mesonLib, simpLib, the BasicProvers shim chain)
Single source file, swept per-chunk in file order: vendor/hol4/src/num/extra_theories/numpairScript.sml

Chunk order (column-0 boundaries, as numeral_sweep chunks them): HEADER(Theory numpair[bare]/Ancestors arithmetic/Libs + the fs/simp/metis_tac helper defs) -> tri_def -> twotri_formula -> tri_formula -> tri_eq_0 -> tri_LT_I -> tri_LT -> tri_11 -> tri_LE -> invtri0_def[OVERRIDE w/ tDefine] -> invtri_def -> (val _ = Unicode invtri) -> invtri0_thm -> SND_invtri0 -> invtri_lower -> invtri_upper -> invtri_linverse -> invtri_unique -> invtri_linverse_r -> tri_le -> invtri_le -> npair_def -> (val _ = set_fixity/Unicode/Overload/TeX block) -> nfst_def -> nsnd_def -> nfst_npair -> nsnd_npair -> npair_cases -> npair -> npair_11 -> nfst_le -> nsnd_le -> npair00 -> npair_EQ_0 -> nfst0 -> nsnd0 -> nfst_le_npair -> nsnd_le_npair -> npair2_lt_E -> npair2_lt_I -> npair2_lt -> npairs_lt_I
Then synthesize structure numpairTheory from Theory.current_theorems() (+ dbTable) and PolyML.export /tmp/hol4_numpair (numeral_sweep tail pattern)
present: TotalDefn.Define / xDefine / tDefine (Y)
TotalDefn.WF_REL_TAC (Y) + Defn.Hol_defn/tgoal/tprove (Y)
numSimps.ARITH_ss + numSimps.REDUCE_ss + numSimps.SUC_FILTER_ss (Y)
reduceLib.REDUCE_CONV (Y)
metisLib.METIS_TAC + mesonLib.ASM_MESON_TAC (Y)
BasicProvers.SRW_TAC / srw_ss / SPOSE_NOT_THEN / Induct_on (the sweep shim) (Y)
prim_recTheory.measure_thm + measure_def + WF_measure + LESS_REFL (Y)
pairTheory.pair_Axiom + pair_induction + pair_case_def + FST + SND + ABS_PAIR_THM + pair_CASES (Y — all that gen_datatype_info needs)
arithmeticTheory: MULT_CLAUSES, LEFT_ADD_DISTRIB, DIV_UNIQUE, LESS_EQ_TRANS, ADD1, LESS_OR_EQ, LESS_TRANS, num_CASES, NOT_LESS, SUB_LESS, SUB_LESS_0 (Y)
boolTheory.EQ_SYM_EQ + EQ_IMP_THM (Y)
Parse.set_fixity + Infixr + overload_on + Unicode.unicode_version + TeX_notation + UTF8.chr + UnicodeChars.sup_minus/sup_1 (Y — Overload/fixity/Unicode block fully works)
Tactic.HO_MATCH_MP_TAC, MATCH_MP_TAC, STRUCT_CASES_TAC, CCONTR_TAC, SUBST_ALL_TAC (Y)
Q.SPECL_THEN, SPEC_THEN, EXISTS_TAC, AP_TERM, ID_SPEC_TAC, SUBGOAL_THEN (Y)
Tactical/boolLib combinators THEN/THENL/>>/>-/\\/rpt + MAP_EVERY=Tactical.MAP_EVERY (Y)
num registered in TypeBase (Y); Theory.current_theorems/current_definitions + DB.theorem/DB.fetch (Y)
VALIDATED LIVE: pair TypeBase registration -> invtri0 tDefine with measure-FST termination tactic -> invtri0_ind registered; twotri_formula proved by Induct_on+SRW_TAC[ARITH_ss]
missing: pair registration in TypeBase — GET: TypeBasePure.gen_datatype_info {ax=pairTheory.pair_Axiom, ind=pairTheory.pair_induction, case_defs=[pairTheory.pair_case_def]} then TypeBase.write, injected in the sweep prelude (right after the existing num registration pattern). Mandatory for invtri0's tupled domain (else Pmatch: 'Pattern (n,a) is not a constructor').

`theorem` (string->thm) — top-level theorem / Theory.theorem absent (n). GET: inject `val theorem = fn nm => #2 (valOf (List.find (fn(n,_)=>n=nm)(Theory.current_theorems()@Theory.current_definitions())))` into the rebind shim (needed for `theorem "invtri0_ind"` at lines 86,95).
numLib.ARITH_TAC / DECIDE / numLib.* — absent (n) but NOT NEEDED (script uses SRW_TAC[ARITH_ss] and a local DECIDE_TAC alias). Do not load numLib.

Auto-termination for invtri0 — Define's guessR is absent in our weak PRIM_STP_TAC shim. GET: override the invtri0_def chunk to call tDefine with the explicit (validated) termination tactic; do NOT try plain Define on it.
blockers: BLOCKER 1 (the layer's keystone): invtri0_def is non-structural recursion with no Termination block; Define auto-termination fails. FIX (validated end-to-end on /tmp/hol4_defn): override chunk `invtri0_def` with TotalDefn.tDefine "invtri0" [QUOTE "invtri0 n a = if n < a + 1 then (n,a) else invtri0 (n - (a + 1)) (a + 1)"] (WF_REL_TAC `measure FST` THEN REWRITE_TAC [prim_recTheory.measure_thm, pairTheory.FST] THEN REPEAT STRIP_TAC THEN BasicProvers.FULL_SIMP_TAC (srw_ss()++ARITH_ss) [arithmeticTheory.NOT_LESS]). Crucial: must use the SWEEP-SHIM BasicProvers.FULL_SIMP_TAC (it injects map ASSUME asl) + NOT_LESS, else the final `n-(a+1)<n` is left as `0<n` because the assumption a+1<=n is dropped. This both proves termination AND registers invtri0_ind (verified).
BLOCKER 2: pair not in TypeBase. FIX: register it in the sweep prelude (see missing). Cheap, mirrors the existing num registration.

BLOCKER 3: `theorem "invtri0_ind"` — no `theorem` function. FIX: inject the current_theorems-search helper (see missing). The sweep stores invtri0_ind via tDefine, so it is in current_theorems by the time invtri0_thm/SND_invtri0 chunks run (file order guarantees invtri0_def precedes them).

MINOR: tactic infixes >>/>-/\\ used by npair2_lt_E/I, nfst_le_npair, nsnd_le_npair. FIX: add `infix >> >- \\` (and keep `infix THEN THENL THEN1 ORELSE; infix 8 by`) to the rebind shim header. boolLib provides the operators; only the fixity declaration is needed per-chunk.

MINOR (flaky, not a hard blocker): 8 METIS_TAC uses (7 theorems: tri_LT, tri_11, invtri_le, npair_EQ_0, nfst0, nsnd0, npair2_lt + the `by metis_tac` in npair2_lt_E). Each is small. Risk = documented mlib time-slice Time-exception under cumulative proving. FIX: per-chunk fail-and-continue (already in the sweep) — re-run any flaky METIS chunk on the exported pass-2 image; or swap to ASM_MESON for the trivial 2-lemma ones if persistent.

POSSIBLE (untested in full, low risk): nfst_le/invtri_unique use the script's weak `DECIDE_TAC = SRW_TAC[ARITH_ss][]` for case splits like `m<n \/ n<m \/ m=n` — SRW_TAC[ARITH_ss] should produce these via the arith dproc, but if a disjunctive split isn't generated, patch those 2-3 chunks to use the num trichotomy lemma (arithmeticTheory has LESS_CASES/LESS_LESS_CASES) explicitly. Validate during the first sweep run.
result.strategy: Script sweep modeled directly on numeral_sweep.sml / arithmetic_sweep.sml, base = /tmp/hol4_defn, target = /tmp/hol4_numpair. Reuse the numeral_sweep harness verbatim (chunker, BasicProvers shim with assumption-injecting ASM_SIMP/FULL_SIMP + Cases_on/Induct_on, the `by`/`suffices_by` infixes, fail-and-continue, current_theorems synthesis + dbTable + export). Changes from numeral_sweep: (1) source path -> numpairScript.sml; (2) theory name "numpair" everywhere (header neutralize, smoke); (3) drop the numeral-specific WF_LESS/TC_BLOCK splices and the ncases/sub_eq'/sub_add' overrides (not referenced here); (4) PRELUDE additions — register pair in TypeBase (gen_datatype_info as above) alongside the inherited num registration, and add the `theorem` helper to the rebind shim; (5) add `infix >> >- \\\\` to the rebind header; (6) ONE chunk override: invtri0_def -> the tDefine-with-explicit-termination string (validated). No splice files needed beyond that single inline override. Smoke before export: check tri_def, npair_def, nfst_npair, npair_11, and `theorem "invtri0_ind"` all resolve. This is a small layer (35 proof obligations) — expect a single sweep pass + maybe one re-run for a flaky METIS chunk. Estimated 1 short session.

result.layer: ind_typeTheory + ind_types.sml (Datatype-package core: define_type / induction+recursion construction) on /tmp/hol4_defn, assuming numpairTheory is built first.
result.summary: Two artifacts. (1) ind_typeScript.sml (~411 lines, Theory ind_type[bare], Ancestors num/prim_rec/arithmetic/numpair) — a THEORY built by SWEEPING the Script: it proves the JRH free-recursive-type machinery (INJN/INJA/INJF/INJP injections into num->'a->bool, ZCONSTR/ZBOT, the ZRECSPACE inductive set via IndDefLib.Hol_reln, the recspace type via new_type_definition, then CONSTR/BOTTOM and the keystones CONSTR_BOT, CONSTR_INJ, CONSTR_IND, CONSTR_REC, FCONS, ISO/ISO_USAGE). (2) ind_types.sml (~1602 lines) — a FILTERED-LOAD SML lib (NOT a theory) that USES ind_typeTheory to implement John Harrison's define_type_raw -> define_type_mutual -> define_type_nested, exported as the single entry point ind_types.define_type : tyspec list -> {induction,recursion}. Datatype.sml:81 binds `val define_type = ind_types.define_type` and `new_asts_datatype = define_type o to_tyspecs` — so ind_types.define_type IS the construction the Datatype package calls. The InductiveDefinition/mesonLib/tautLib/Prim_rec/simp/TypeBase substrate is ALL present on hol4_defn; only three concrete gaps + the standard shims stand in the way. Probed extensively: 60+ name checks across 8 probe runs.
modules: PREREQ (other agent): build numpairTheory by sweeping vendor/hol4/src/num/extra_theories/numpairScript.sml (Ancestors: arithmetic; Libs: TotalDefn numSimps numLib metisLib) on /tmp/hol4_numsimps or /tmp/hol4_defn -> /tmp/hol4_numpair; provides nfst_npair/nsnd_npair/numpairSyntax

STEP A — sweep vendor/hol4/src/datatype/ind_typeScript.sml on /tmp/hol4_numpair (or hol4_defn+numpair), column-0 chunk at Theorem/Definition/val/local boundaries, HOLSource.inputFile each chunk, PolyML.use fail-and-continue, then synthesize structure ind_typeTheory from Theory.current_{theorems,definitions,axioms}() -> export /tmp/hol4_ind_type
STEP B — filtered-load vendor/hol4/src/datatype/ind_types.sig then ind_types.sml (.sig then HOLSource-filtered .sml, useFilt pattern) on /tmp/hol4_ind_type -> export /tmp/hol4_ind_types
(downstream, NOT this layer) ParseDatatype.sml, RecordType.sml, DataSize.sml, then Datatype.sml assembling Hol_datatype/Datatype

IndDefLib.Hol_reln (ZRECSPACE inductive set)
Prim_rec.EXISTS_EQUATION / INDUCT_THEN / prove_cases_thm / prove_constructors_one_one / prove_constructors_distinct / prove_rec_fn_exists

num IS registered in TypeBase (induction_of/axiom_of :num succeed)
sumTheory.sum_Axiom/sum_INDUCT/sum_case_def/OUTL/OUTR; sumSyntax

arithmeticTheory.EVEN/EVEN_DOUBLE/EQ_MULT_LCANCEL; prim_recTheory.INV_SUC_EQ (bare via open); numTheory.NOT_SUC/INV_SUC

Rewrite.GEN_REWRITE_TAC/empty_rewrites; Conv.AC_CONV; boolSyntax.T/mk_arb/strip_comb
TypeBase.read/induction_of/axiom_of, TypeBasePure.gen_datatype_info, TypeBase.write
Rsyntax.new_specification, Definition.new_specification/new_definition, OpenTheoryMap.OpenTheory_const_name, Parse.remove_ovl_mapping
Sweep infra: Theory.current_theorems/new_theory/register_replayed_axiom, Parse.current_grammars/parse_from_grammars, HOLSource.inputFile, Globals.interactive/checking_type_names, Lib.with_flag
TotalDefn.Define (numpair build uses it)
missing: numpairTheory / nfst_npair / nsnd_npair / numpairSyntax — NOT present (this is the prerequisite layer below me; ind_typeScript line 35 does `CONJ (SPEC_ALL nfst_npair) (SPEC_ALL nsnd_npair)`). Get it: sweep numpairScript.sml first (other agent).

UNIQUE_SKOLEM_ALT (boolTheory/boolLib) — NOT bound on hol4_defn. ind_typeScript CONSTR_REC rewrites with it (line 317). Get it: prove inline before the CONSTR_REC chunk — VERIFIED provable via `boolLib.prove(Parse.Term[QUOTE "!P:'a->'b->bool. (!x. ?!y. P x y) = (?f. !x y. (P x y = (f x = y)))"], mesonLib.MESON_TAC[])`. (Its boolLib derivation via EXISTS_UNIQUE_ALT won't work — EXISTS_UNIQUE_ALT is also unbound; MESON is the route.)

structure numLib — NOT present. ind_typeScript uses numLib.INDUCT_TAC (FCONS_UNDO[local], droppable) and numLib.arith_ss (FCONS_DEST). Get it: prelude shim `structure numLib = struct val INDUCT_TAC = Prim_rec.INDUCT_THEN numTheory.INDUCTION Tactic.ASSUME_TAC; val arith_ss = <hol_ss as defined at top of script> end` (INDUCT_THEN over numTheory.INDUCTION VERIFIED working).

sum NOT in TypeBase (`unable to find sum$sum in the TypeBase`). ind_types.sml lines 762-763 do TypeBase.induction_of/axiom_of `:'a+'b` AT LOAD TIME (top-level vals) -> raises immediately. Get it: register sum into TypeBase in the ind_types.sml prelude — VERIFIED: `TypeBase.write (TypeBasePure.gen_datatype_info {ax=sumTheory.sum_Axiom, ind=sumTheory.sum_INDUCT, case_defs=[sumTheory.sum_case_def]})` then induction_of `:'a+'b` succeeds. (Mirror build_defn's num registration.)
Lib.trace — NOT bound (ind_types.sml define_type_nested wrapper: `defnested |> trace ("Vartype Format Complaint",0)`). Get it: shim `fun trace _ f = f` or drop the |> trace pipe in a modPatch.
blockers: grammarDB returns NONE for ancestor segments (KNOWN shim). ind_types.sml line 30: `val SOME ind_type_grammars = grammarDB {thyname="ind_type"}` -> raises Bind. FIX (standard): modPatch replace with `Parse.current_grammars ()` and drop the `val SOME ... =` to `val ind_type_grammars = Parse.current_grammars ()` (exactly the Extract/TotalDefn grammarDB patch in build_defn_checkpoint.sml lines 47-66). Same in ind_typeScript if any chunk references grammarDB (none seen — it's a [bare] theory).

sum-not-in-TypeBase at ind_types.sml LOAD TIME (lines 762-763 are top-level vals, fire before any function call). This is the single hardest gate to loading ind_types.sml. FIX: register sum (and keep num registered) in the prelude BEFORE useFilt ind_types.sml — verified recipe above. Without it the structure won't even compile-load.

UNIQUE_SKOLEM_ALT missing breaks CONSTR_REC (the recursion theorem — the deepest proof in ind_typeScript, ~45 lines). FIX: splice a `val UNIQUE_SKOLEM_ALT = boolLib.prove(... , MESON_TAC[])` before the CONSTR_REC chunk runs (inject after the header rebind shim, like arithmetic_sweep's TC_BLOCK splice). Verified provable by MESON.

HARD-but-mechanical: CONSTR_REC also leans on prove_nonschematic_inductive_relations_exist + a hand `new_basic_type_definition` (local fun, lines 165-182) + SKOLEM_THM/EXISTS_UNIQUE_THM rewriting. All substrate present; risk is proof-tactic fidelity (ASM_SIMP/SIMP_RULE behaviour) — if a chunk FAILs, fall back to a hand-splice in hol4_support/ind_type_splices/ (same pattern as arith_splices). Budget the sweep generously (--max-steps 2-3e12); CONSTR_REC + CONSTR_IND are the step-heavy ones.

ind_types.sml is a LARGE single structure (1602 lines, define_type_nested mutual recursion). An uncaught exception in ANY top-level val kills the whole filtered PolyML.use (the known per-decl-vs-whole-file hazard). Because it's `structure ind_types :> ind_types = struct ... end` it MUST load as one unit (can't per-decl split a sealed structure). So every top-level val (esp. the two TypeBase calls + the DE_EXISTENTIALIZE_RULE `prove`d pth at line 1028) must succeed. Pre-register sum, ensure num stays, and the `prove` at 1028 needs SELECT_AX (present) — should be clean.
numpair itself is a non-trivial sweep (DECIDE_TAC=SRW[ARITH_ss], heavy METIS/SRW, BasicProvers shim) — flagged for the prerequisite agent, not blocking my layer's design but gating its start.
result.strategy: SWEEP for ind_typeScript.sml (it's a Theory[bare] -> chunk-and-synthesize, exactly like numeral/arithmetic_sweep), then FILTERED-LOAD for ind_types.sml (it's a sealed SML structure -> .sig then HOLSource-filtered .sml via useFilt, NOT a theory). Concretely: (A) Build numpair first (prereq). (B) New script build_ind_type_checkpoint.sml on /tmp/hol4_numpair: set Globals.interactive:=true; register replayed axioms; install the rebind/BasicProvers/boolLib shims from arithmetic_sweep; add a `structure numLib` shim (INDUCT_TAC + arith_ss=hol_ss) and a `fun trace _ f = f` shim; splice `UNIQUE_SKOLEM_ALT` (MESON-proved) into scope before sweeping; sweep ind_typeScript with isBoundary chunking + inLocal handling (the file has 3 column-0 `local` blocks: recspace_tydef at 165, FCONS_UNDO context, OpenTheoryMap at 402 — must NOT split); the IndDefLib.Hol_reln ZRECSPACE chunk and the `local ... new_basic_type_definition ... in val recspace_tydef` chunk are the structural keystones — keep them whole; synthesize `structure ind_typeTheory` from current_theorems/definitions (+ the recspace type is a real new_type_definition so it lives in the segment); export /tmp/hol4_ind_type. (C) build_ind_types_checkpoint.sml on /tmp/hol4_ind_type: prelude registers sum into TypeBase (gen_datatype_info from sumTheory) — CRITICAL, must precede the load; modPatch the grammarDB line -> Parse.current_grammars(); useFilt the .sig then .sml as ONE unit; smoke-test `ind_types.define_type [(Type[QUOTE\":'foo\"], [(\"C1\",[]),(\"C2\",[Type[QUOTE\":num\"]])])]` returns {induction,recursion}; export /tmp/hol4_ind_types. Reuse harnesses verbatim where possible: arithmetic_sweep.sml for the sweep skeleton, build_defn_checkpoint.sml useFilt for the lib load.
result.confidence: Medium-high. All four substrate families (InductiveDefinition, mesonLib/tautLib, Prim_rec, simp/TypeBase) are present and the three real gaps (UNIQUE_SKOLEM_ALT, sum-TypeBase, numLib) each have a VERIFIED fix (each probed and confirmed working on hol4_defn). The grammarDB and shim patterns are well-trodden. Two residual risks keep it off "high": (1) ind_typeScript's CONSTR_REC/CONSTR_IND proofs are tactic-heavy and may need hand-splices if ASM_SIMP/SIMP_RULE fidelity diverges (mitigated by the established arith_splices fallback); (2) numpair is a genuine prerequisite build I did not exercise (assumed per the task). The Datatype-package entry point (ind_types.define_type) and its load-time TypeBase[sum] dependency are pinned down precisely.

result.summary: Goal layer: make `Datatype.Datatype [QUOTE "tree = Leaf | Node tree tree"]` work on /tmp/hol4_defn. The smoke type is NON-enum (Node carries args), so it routes through `build_tyinfos (new_asts_datatype astl)` -> `ind_types.define_type` -> `ind_typeTheory` (CONSTR/FCONS/BOTTOM/recspace/INJN..INJP), then `Prim_rec.define_case_constant`, `TypeBasePure.gen_datatype_info`, `DataSize.define_size`, and `persistent_tyinfo` (TypeBase.export + save_thms + Parse.overload_on "case" + computeLib.write_datatype_info). The enum path (EnumType) and RecordType path are not exercised by the smoke type, but both modules must COMPILE because Datatype.sml `open`s DataSize and references EnumType/RecordType. THE DOMINANT BLOCKER is the ind_type theory ancestry: `ind_typeScript` declares Ancestors `num prim_rec arithmetic numpair`, and `numpairTheory` is NOT on the checkpoint — it must be swept first. numpair needs `numLib` (also absent), which in turn needs `sumSimps`/`combinSimps`/`optionSimps` (absent). Everything ELSE the layer needs is already present: real TypeBase, Prim_rec.prove_cases_thm/prove_constructors_one_one/prove_rec_fn_exists/new_recursive_definition, InductiveDefinition.new_inductive_definition, srw_ss/SRW_TAC, ARITH_ss/REDUCE_ss, reduceLib/Arithconv/computeLib.CBV_CONV, mesonLib/metisLib, computeLib.write_datatype_info, Defn/TotalDefn.Define. ENTRYPOINT ANSWER: use `Datatype.Datatype` (NOT bossLib.Datatype — bossLib is not loaded), argument is a plain `Type.hol_type frag list` = `[QUOTE "tree = Leaf | Node tree tree"]`; NO backtick parsing and NO pre-built ParseDatatype.AST is required — Datatype.Datatype internally calls `ParseDatatype.hparse (type_grammar()) q` which parses the bar-grammar ("Node tree tree", not "Node of tree => tree"). `Datatype.Hol_datatype` is the legacy variant using `parse` (the "of"/"=>" grammar). For maximum robustness one may bypass parsing by calling `Datatype.astHol_datatype [("tree", Constructors [("Leaf",[]),("Node",[dVartype "tree", dVartype "tree"])])]` with an explicit AST (after `open ParseDatatype_dtype`), since recursive occurrences are encoded as the type's own tyvar.

blockers: BLOCKER 1 (dominant): numpairTheory ancestry. ind_typeScript Ancestors = num prim_rec arithmetic numpair; numpair absent. FIX: sweep numpairScript (Script->Theory) using arithmetic_sweep.sml as template — chunk at column-0 boundaries, HOLSource-filter each chunk, PolyML.use fail-and-continue, synthesize structure numpairTheory from Theory.current_theorems(). It uses srw_ss()/ARITH_ss/METIS_TAC (all present) + Define (present). Watch the METIS cumulative Time-exception (CLAUDE.md caveat) — split METIS-heavy chunks or swap small METIS_PROVE->MESON_TAC. This is the biggest structural risk per the Stage-6 roadmap.

BLOCKER 2: numLib eager-val load failure. numLib std_ss/arith_ss ++ sumSimps.SUM_ss/combinSimps.COMBIN_ss/optionSimps.OPTION_ss (all absent). FIX: load filtered sumSimps, combinSimps, optionSimps BEFORE numLib. If any of those won't load cleanly, an alternative is to splice a numLib variant whose std_ss/arith_ss are lazified (wrap in `fn () => ...`), but loading the three small frags is cleaner.

BLOCKER 3: grammarDB returns NONE for ancestor segments. ind_types.sml line uses `val SOME ind_type_grammars = grammarDB {thyname="ind_type"}`; Datatype.sml line 70 `val SOME arithmetic_grammars = grammarDB {thyname="arithmetic"}`; EnumType line 18 same. After sweeping, grammarDB{ind_type} may be NONE on reload. FIX (known shim, build_defn already does this for arithmetic/bool): post-filter modPatch replacing `val SOME X_grammars = grammarDB {thyname=...}` with `val X_grammars = Parse.current_grammars ()` (or temp_set_grammars to current). Apply to ind_types, Datatype, EnumType.

BLOCKER 4: backtick quotations in the Script sweeps. numpairScript/ind_typeScript use ``...`` term/type quotations. FIX: HOLSource.inputFile converts ``...`` to [QUOTE ...] frag lists (the standard sweep filter) — already the project's method; just route every chunk through it.

BLOCKER 5: persistent_tyinfo side effects. astHol_datatype calls Theory.scrub() + datatype_thm (save_thm) + TypeBase.export + save_thms (save_thm) + Parse.overload_on + computeLib.write_datatype_info. All deps present; computeLib.write_datatype_info=Y. RISK: an export/save path that hits OS.FileSys finalize could trip the exn-unwinding VM halt as seen with export_theory. MITIGATION: Globals.interactive:=true is already set in build_defn; save_thm to the in-memory segment is fine (markerTheory etc. did this). If overload_on/scrub raises, wrap astHol_datatype body or use primHol_datatype (no persistence) for a first smoke, then add persistence.

BLOCKER 6 (avoidance): DB.fetch baked stub RAISES. numpairScript/ind_typeScript or their simp-set restores may call DB.fetch by name. FIX (known): shadow structure DB with a fetchTable + log-and-return-TRUTH fallback (exactly as build_defn_checkpoint.sml lines 259-272). Carry that shadow into this build script.
result.strategy: Mixed: SWEEP two theories (numpairScript, ind_typeScript) + FILTERED-LOAD ~10 SML lib modules, on a new build script that opens on /tmp/hol4_defn and exports /tmp/hol4_datatype. Concrete order: (Phase A, filtered libs) sumSimps -> combinSimps -> optionSimps -> numLib -> ParseDatatype_dtype -> ParseDatatype. (Phase B, sweep) numpairScript -> structure numpairTheory; then ind_typeScript -> structure ind_typeTheory (use numeral_sweep.sml/arithmetic_sweep.sml as the harness template: chunkPatches, BasicProvers shim already exists on this image, grammarDB->current_grammars patch, DB.fetch shadow). (Phase C, filtered libs) ind_types -> DataSize -> EnumType -> RecordType -> DatatypeSimps -> Datatype. (Phase D, smoke) `open ParseDatatype_dtype; Datatype.Datatype [QUOTE "tree = Leaf | Node tree tree"]` (or the AST-direct astHol_datatype form to bypass parsing entirely), then assert TypeBase.fetch of the new "tree" tyinfo and inspect the generated tree_induction / tree_Axiom / tree_11 / tree_distinct / tree_nchotomy / tree_size_def. Reuse build_defn's modPatches/useFilt/DB-shadow verbatim. Recommend a FIRST smoke via primHol_datatype (TypeBase.theTypeBase()) to validate the ind_types core before turning on persistent_tyinfo's export/save side effects. ENTRYPOINT/ARG (the requested answer): function = Datatype.Datatype; argument type = Type.hol_type frag list; literal = [QUOTE "tree = Leaf | Node tree tree"]; recursion encoded by reusing the type name as an atomic type ("Node tree tree"); NO backticks, NO pre-parsed AST needed (Datatype.Datatype calls ParseDatatype.hparse internally). Datatype.Hol_datatype is the legacy "of/=>"-grammar twin.
result.confidence: medium-high on the entrypoint/arg form and the present-vs-missing inventory (directly probed); medium on numpairScript sweeping cleanly in one pass (heaviest item, 82 METIS/SIMP calls, the known Time-exception caveat and possible srw_ss coupling); medium-high that ind_typeScript + the support libs follow once numpair+numLib land, since every non-numpair dependency (TypeBase, Prim_rec, InductiveDefinition, computeLib.write_datatype_info, provers) is already confirmed present on /tmp/hol4_defn.

result.summary: Definitive HAVE/MISSING inventory for the numpair->ind_type->Datatype path, probed against /tmp/hol4_defn (16 probe drivers, functional smoke tests, not just name lookups). The checkpoint is a STRONG base: it already carries everything the three layers consume from BELOW — real TypeBase/TypeBasePure registry (gen_datatype_info, mk_datatype_info, mk_recordtype_*, add_extra, put_simpls, put_size, COPY, export), full Prim_rec case/induction/recursion provers (prove_cases_thm, prove_induction_thm, prove_rec_fn_exists, prove_case_eq_thm, case_cong_thm, define_new_type_bijections), kernel new_type_definition/new_specification (FUNCTIONALLY VERIFIED end-to-end), IndDefLib.Hol_reln + InductiveDefinition.prove_nonschematic_inductive_relations_exist + bool_monoset (Hol_reln RUNS — ind_type's ZRECSPACE is reachable), full TFL/TotalDefn.Define + tDefine + WF_REL_TAC + tgoal/tprove, srw_ss()++ARITH_ss (discharges arith goals), MESON/METIS/TAUT, sum/option/basicSize/pair/num/arith/prim_rec theories, all notation infra (Unicode, TeX_notation, set_fixity, Overload, OpenTheoryMap, remove_ovl_mapping). The three target structures (numpairTheory, ind_typeTheory/ind_types, Datatype) and their support modules (EnumType, DataSize, ParseDatatype, ParseDatatype_dtype, RecordType, bossLib.Datatype) are all ABSENT (expected — this layer builds them). Build order: numpairScript (Definition-sweep) -> ind_typeScript (Definition-sweep) -> ind_types.sml/EnumType.sml/DataSize.sml/RecordType.sml (filtered SML loads) -> Datatype.sml (filtered) which defines Datatype.Datatype; then bossLib.Datatype = Datatype.Datatype re-export. FIRST WALLS, one per layer: (numpair) auto-termination FAILS on the non-structural invtri0_def -> needs a tDefine/WF_REL_TAC splice; (ind_type) grammarDB{ind_type}/{arithmetic} returns NONE so `val SOME g = grammarDB{...}` raises Bind -> the standard current_grammars patch; (Datatype) ParseDatatype_dtype is missing and must be loaded before ParseDatatype.

VERIFIED grammarDB{arithmetic} returns NONE (functional probe GRAMMAR_ARITH=NONE; the name-probe Y is misleading — it's a total function returning NONE), and `val SOME g = grammarDB{...}` raises Bind (SOME_GRAMMAR_BIND=BIND_RAISED). ind_typeScript itself doesn't fix a grammar, but ind_types.sml/EnumType.sml DO. FIX: the established modPatch — replace `val SOME X_grammars = grammarDB{thyname=...}` with `val X_grammars = Parse.current_grammars()` in ind_types.sml and EnumType.sml (same patch already used for Extract/TotalDefn in build_defn_checkpoint).

FIX: load src/parse/ParseDatatype_dtype (.sml, likely .sig too) via useFilt BEFORE ParseDatatype.sml. Also ensure RecordType + EnumType + DataSize + ind_types load before Datatype.sml (Datatype opens DataSize, uses RecordType/EnumType/ind_types).

SECONDARY ind_type: CONSTR_REC is the single heaviest proof (prove_nonschematic_inductive_relations_exist + many SIMP/MESON steps). Risk = mlib/MESON cumulative Time-exception (CLAUDE.md caveat: prove one goal-group per process). If CONSTR_REC throws a spurious Time exn, splice it into its own fresh-image sub-run or split the sweep so it runs early with a fresh step budget. Give the ind_type sweep a generous --max-steps (>=30e9).

INTERPRETER/RTS concern: inductive-type construction stresses LARGE TERM building (ind_type's recspace = num->'a->bool encoding; CONSTR_REC's ?!y SKOLEM proof; ZRECSPACE_INDUCT) and DEEP nested SIMP/MESON recursion. The MESON 'solve_goal: Too deep' seen in one probe is a normal MESON depth bound, not a runtime fault. No NEW RTS primitive is implicated — Hol_reln, new_type_definition, define_new_type_bijections, prove_nonschematic_inductive_relations_exist all executed cleanly. Main runtime risk is step-budget + the known MESON/mlib per-process Time-exception, both mitigated by fresh-image splicing.
